title: Negation & Questions
intro: Asking questions and saying 'no' in Spanish is simpler than in English — no 'do/does' needed! Just add 'no' or change your intonation.

sections.title: Negation — just add 'no' before the verb
sections.content:
Put 'no' directly before the verb — nothing else changes:
• Hablo inglés. → No hablo inglés. (I don't speak English.)
• Tengo coche. → No tengo coche. (I don't have a car.)

Spanish allows — and requires! — double negatives:
• No tengo nada. (I don't have anything.)
• No voy nunca. (I never go.)
• No conozco a nadie. (I don't know anyone.)

sections.title: Asking questions — no 'do/does' needed
sections.content:
In Spanish you can make a question by:
1. Changing your intonation (rising tone): ¿Hablas español?
2. Swapping verb and subject: ¿Hablas tú español?
There's no Spanish equivalent of 'do/does' — just use the verb directly!

sections.label: ¡ ¿ — Unique to Spanish!
sections.content:
Spanish adds an upside-down mark at the START of questions and exclamations:
• ¿Hablas español? (Do you speak Spanish?)
• ¡Hola! (Hello!)
In typing or writing, always include both marks.

questions.explanation: In Spanish, just put 'no' before the verb. No hablo inglés ✓
¿Por qué? = Why? ✓ (Porque = Because — the answer word)

questions.prompt: ¿___ cuesta esto? (How much does this cost?)
questions.explanation: ¿Cuánto cuesta? = How much does it cost? ✓

questions.prompt: 'No tengo nada.' This uses a double negative. Is this:
questions.explanation: Double negatives are correct in Spanish! 'No tengo nada' = I don't have anything ✓
¿Cómo estás? = How are you? ✓ (also used to mean 'what's it like?')
